channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Bluesky finds with growth comes growing pains — and bots
item.description: Bluesky has seen its user base soar since the U.S. presidential election, boosted by people seeking refuge from Elon Musk’s X, or wanting an alternative to Meta’s Threads and its algorithms.
item.dc:creator: Ali Swenson And Barara Ortutay, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pickup truck driver killed by police after driving through Texas mall and injuring 5
item.description: KILLEEN, Texas (AP) — A pickup truck driver fleeing police careened through the doors of a JCPenney store in Texas and continued through a busy mall, injuring five people before he was fatally shot by officers, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: China says US is 'playing with fire' after latest military aid for Taiwan
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — The Chinese government protested Sunday the latest American announcements of military sales and assistance to Taiwan, warning the United States that it is “playing with fire.” U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Suspect shot, killed after driving truck into Texas mall in incident that left at least 5 injured: police
item.description: At least five people were injured, and the suspect killed, after a man drove his pickup truck through the glass doors of a J.C. Penney at the Killeen Mall in Texas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lara Trump says she will not seek to replace Rubio in Senate
item.description: The incoming president announced Rubio as his Secretary of State choice in November. Earlier this month, Lara Trump stepped down as RNC co-chair to “heavily consider” taking on the role in Congress's upper chamber.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KREX 5

item.title: Albania to close TikTok for a year blaming it for promoting violence among children
item.description: Albanian authorities held 1,300 meetings with teachers and parents following the stabbing death of a teenager in mid-November by another teen after a quarrel that started on TikTok.
item.dc:creator: Llazar Semini, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mahomes throws for TD and runs for score as Chiefs beat Texans 27-19 to close in on No. 1 seed
item.description: Patrick Mahomes knew he could run on the ankle he sprained last week. The bigger concern for the Chiefs quarterback was how he would stop, when all the weight and pressure and momentum would land squarely upon it.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Amazon and Starbucks workers are on strike. Trump might have something to do with it
item.description: Amazon delivery drivers and Starbucks baristas are on strike in a handful of U.S. cities as they seek to exert pressure on the two major companies to recognize them as unionized employees or to meet demands for an inaugural labor contract.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Florida man caught on camera grabbing 16-year-old's butt at Chick-fil-A
item.description: A Florida man was arrested on Thursday after a security camera at a Chick-fil-A restaurant captured him grabbing a 16 year-old girl's buttocks.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, Fox 5

item.title: Biden signs bill that averts a government shutdown and brings a close to days of Washington upheaval
item.description: President Joe Biden signed a bill into law Saturday that averts a government shutdown, bringing a final close to days of upheaval after Congress approved a temporary funding plan just past the deadline and refused President-elect Donald Trump’s core debt demands in the package.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, FARNOUSH AMIRI and MATT BROWN Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Here are the 34 Republicans that voted against the funding bill to avoid shutdown
item.description: More than 30 House Republicans voted against the government funding bill to avert a shutdown on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Senate passes RFK Stadium land bill, giving the Washington Commanders a major off-the-field win
item.description: The path for the NFL's Washington Commanders to return to the nation’s capital is clear after an on-again, off-again saga in Congress ended early Saturday with a postmidnight reprieve.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Top-ranking NYPD officer abruptly resigns amid sexual misconduct allegations
item.description: The top uniformed police officer in the New York Police Department has resigned amid allegations he demanded sex from a subordinate.
item.dc:creator: Philip Marcelo, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 5 of the most frustrating health insurer tactics and why they exist
item.description: Even for those with health insurance, coverage does not ensure access to care, much less high-quality and affordable care.
item.dc:creator: Monica S. Aswani Assistant Professor of Health Services Administration, University of Alabama at Birmingham, ABC 4

item.title: Daughter of notorious 'pom-pom mom' says cheerleading murder plot nearly destroyed her life
item.description: In 1991, Wanda Holloway was charged with conspiring to kill the mother of her daughter’s rival. Holloway's daughter, Shanna, is speaking out in a new true crime docuseries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NORAD's Santa tracker was a Cold War morale boost. Now it attracts millions of kids
item.description: The Christmas tradition has become nearly global in scope: Children from around the world track Santa Claus as he sweeps across the earth, delivering presents and defying time. Each year, at least 100,000 kids call into the North American Aerospace Defense Command to inquire about Santa’s location.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate sends spending deal to Biden's desk, averting shutdown
item.description: The Senate in the early hours of Saturday passed a stopgap funding package, avoiding a government shutdown that would have furloughed hundreds of thousands of federal workers and bringing a tumultuous week in Congress to a close.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KTLA 5

item.title: Senate passes bill to boost Social Security benefits for some
item.description: The Senate voted overwhelmingly in the early hours of Saturday to pass legislation to boost Social Security benefits for more than 2 million Americans.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KTLA 5

item.title: Santa and Mrs. Claus use military transports to bring Christmas to an Alaska Native village
item.description: YAKUTAT, Alaska (AP) — Forget the open-air sleigh overloaded with gifts and powered by flying reindeer. Santa and Mrs.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Missouri governor commutes sentence of white police officer convicted of fatally shooting Black man
item.description: JEFFERSON CITY, Mo. (AP) — A white former Kansas City police officer who was convicted of involuntary manslaughter in the fatal shooting of a Black man was released from prison Friday after Missouri’s governor commuted his sentence to parole. The decision by Republican Gov.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Oregon sheriff concerned about letters asking people to track possible immigrants
item.description: LINCOLN CITY, Ore. (AP) — A sheriff in Oregon says he has contacted the FBI and is increasingly concerned about a letter circulating in his rural, coastal community asking people to write down the license plate numbers of possible immigrants.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: University of California campuses resolve discrimination complaints stemming from Gaza protests
item.description: The U.S. Department of Education said Friday it has reached an agreement with the University of California system resolving complaints from Jewish and Muslim students of discrimination and harassment during protests last spring over the war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How a federal government shutdown could impact Californians
item.description: (KTLA) — The federal government could shut down at 12:01 a.m. Saturday unless Congress agrees on a new spending measure. For most Californians, the impact of a shutdown wouldn’t be felt immediately; services like Social Security, veterans, and unemployment benefits will still be distributed.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Obama reveals his favorite books of 2024
item.description: With 2024 coming to a close, former President Obama is again sharing out a list of his favorites, posting his ten favorite books of the year.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, Fox 5

item.title: Party City going out of business, starting process 'immediately': report
item.description: Party City, once among the top names in party-supply retailers, is going out of business, according to an announcement from CEO Barry Litwin that was viewed by CNN.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: US military orders new safety guidelines for key Osprey part after near-crash
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The military has ordered new safety guidelines for a key part in all Osprey aircraft after a recent accident revealed the same problem that caused a fatal crash in Japan last year. During a Nov.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wisconsin school shooting by first-semester student lasted 8 minutes: official
item.description: The 15-year-old girl who police say killed a teacher and teen student in a shooting at a Christian school Wisconsin was in her first semester at the school and the attack lasted eight minutes, school officials say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tesla recalling almost 700,000 vehicles due to tire pressure monitoring system issue
item.description: (AP) -- Tesla is recalling almost 700,000 vehicles because of an issue with the warning light on the tire pressure monitoring system.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Starbucks workers plan strikes that could hit hundreds of stores by Christmas Eve
item.description: Workers at Starbucks stores plan to go on a five-day strike starting Friday to protest lack of progress in contract negotiations with the company.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: FAA temporarily restricts drone flights in New York amid concerns over recently reported sightings
item.description: The FAA issued temporary restrictions on drone flights in 27 areas of New York in response to the influx of reported sightings in recent weeks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Immigration drives highest US population growth in over 2 decades
item.description: The U.S. Census Bureau released a report Thursday showing that immigration drove the U.S. population's highest rate increase in over two decades.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Farmers, business owners, fire survivors face uncertainty after $100B in disaster relief flounders
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — American farmers, small business owners and wildfire survivors are among those who will suffer if Congress cannot agree on a new spending bill after President-elect Donald Trump abruptly rejected a bipartisan plan that included more than $100 billion in disaster aid.
item.dc:creator: TRAVIS LOLLER, LEAH WILLINGHAM and JENNIFER SINCO KELLEHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Michigan school shooter loses bid to withdraw guilty plea in 4 deaths
item.description: PONTIAC, Mich. (AP) — A judge on Thursday refused to allow a Michigan school shooter to withdraw his guilty plea to two dozen charges, including terrorism and first-degree murder.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 1 dead in small plane crash near home of NFL player Dion Dawkins
item.description: One person died in a small plane crash Thursday morning close to the house of Buffalo Bills offensive lineman Dion Dawkins, police said.
item.dc:creator: Aidan Joly, KRON4

item.title: How drone activity could affect US holiday travel plans
item.description: Recent drone sightings around the country have raised concerns over whether drone activity could affect holiday travel
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, KRON4

item.title: In Florida, a race is on to save the Everglades and protect a key source of drinking water
item.description: EVERGLADES, Fla. (AP) — In a region of Florida known as the River of Grass, John Kominoski plops into hip-deep waters. Blobs of brown periphyton – a mishmash of algae, bacteria and other organisms – carpet the surface.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA and REBECCA BLACKWELL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump names Herschel Walker as ambassador to Bahamas
item.description: Trump had quipped before Election Day that he would put Walker in charge of a new missile defense shield if elected.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9
